items.title: Ongeloof bij Brugge: 'We waren beter, geweldig zelfs en brachten Atlético paniek'
items.summary: Club Brugge werd dinsdagavond uitgeschakeld in de Champions League. In de return van de tussenronde was Atlético Madrid te sterk , nadat het eerder 3-3 was geworden. De Belgische topclub snapte er na afloop niets van, hoe het mis had kunnen gaan.

items.title: VI Live: Bodo/Glimt verdubbelt voorsprong in Milaan en gaat het flikken
items.summary: Internazionale hoopt dinsdagavond de 3-1 uitnederlaag tegen FK Bodø/Glimt weg te poetsen. Verder heeft Club Brugge op bezoek bij Atlético Madrid kans om de achtste finale te halen. Volg de duels in de tussenronde van de Champions League in deze VI Live.

items.title: WC host cities warn Congress of security concerns
items.summary: Local officials representing World Cup host cities warned Congress on Tuesday that security concerns, coordination problems and inadequate federal funding are risking catastrophe with little more than 100 days remaining before the games begin.

items.title: PSG's Hakimi to stand trial over rape allegation
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi insists he will "allow the truth to come out publicly" after learning he is to face trial for an alleged rape.

items.title: Cardoso scores first UCL goal on 'perfect' night
items.summary: United States midfielder Johnny Cardoso said he had a "perfect" night on Tuesday after scoring in Atlético Madrid's 4-1 Champions League playoff second leg win over Club Brugge, as the LaLiga side cruised into the competition's round of 16.

items.title: Refs say VAR missed foul on Koundé in Barça loss
items.summary: Girona's winning goal against Barcelona last week should have been disallowed, the Spanish Referee's Committee [CTA] said on Tuesday.

items.title: Newcastle v Qarabag, Inter v Bodø/Glimt: Champions League playoff second legs – live
items.summary: ⚽ Champions League updates from the 8pm GMT kick-offs ⚽ Live scores | Sign up for Football Daily | And mail Scott A short-corner routine by Atleti is pinged back down the left flank. Matteo Ruggeri crosses, the ball dropping onto the foot of Alexander Sørloth, who slots with a confident sidefoot from close range past the rooted Simon Mignolet. That’s a hat-trick for Sørloth, and Atleti will play either Liverpool or Tottenham Hotspur in the last 16. … so having said that, Atletico establish a two-goal lead, and are surely in the hat for Friday’s last-16 draw. Marcos Llorente plays a long ball down the inside-right channel for Alexander Sørloth, who should release Ademola Lookman into the box down the left. His pass across is poor, behind Lookman, but Antoine Griezmann comes over, offering himself as a wall for Lookman to ping the ball off. A one-two down the left, then Lookman crosses low. Sørloth can’t miss from six yards and that is surely that! Continue reading...

items.title: Champions League: Sørloth’s hat-trick guides Atlético Madrid past Club Brugge
items.summary: La Liga side win 4-1 on the night, 7-4 on aggregate Atlético will face Liverpool or Tottenham in last 16 A hat-trick by Alexander Sørloth helped Atlético Madrid finally shake off Club Brugge and advance to the Champions League round of 16. The La Liga side won 4-1 on Tuesday to complete a 7-4 aggregate victory. But the wide margin on the night didn’t tell the full story of Brugge’s resilience over two legs. A 3-3 draw in Belgium last week had seen Brugge come back from two goals down and then level again in the 89th minute. More to follow Continue reading...

items.title: 'Bondscoach Marokko stapt op, Barça denkt aan City-aanvaller'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Champions League live updates: Bodø/Glimt stunning last year's finalists Inter
items.summary: Enjoy all the action from today's UEFA Champions League matches, including Internazionale vs. Bodø/Glimt and Newcastle United vs. Qarabag.

items.title: ADO wint knap bij Willem II, ook Cambuur stap dichter bij Eredivisie
items.summary: ADO Den Haag heeft goede zaken gedaan in de top van de Keuken Kampioen Divisie. Op bezoek bij Willem II werd gewonnen, waardoor een nieuwe stap naar de Eredivisie gezet werd. Dezelfde vlieger ging op voor SC Cambuur, dat afrekende met FC Eindhoven.

items.title: World Cup host cities ‘running out of time’ with $625m in funding held up by shutdown
items.summary: Funds promised for security have not been received Officials warn of potential cancellations to fan festivals Local and national officials expressed concern on Tuesday that the ongoing partial government shutdown in the United States could adversely affect planning and preparation for the 2026 World Cup, which is just over 100 days away. In a hearing before the House committee on homeland security, representatives from Miami, Kansas City and New Jersey – three locations that will host a combined total of 21 matches in the tournament, including the final – said they are still waiting on federal funds to be released to their respective local agencies. Last July, lawmakers pledged $625m in federal assistance toward World Cup security via the Trump administration’s “big beautiful” policy bill. Continue reading...

items.title: Prestianni traint ondanks voorlopige schorsing mee bij Benfica
items.summary: Benfica-speler Gianluca Prestianni werd maandag voorlopig geschorst door de UEFA vanwege vermeend racisme aan het adres van Real Madrid-sterspeler Vinícius Júnior tijdens het eerste duel tussen beide clubs. Richting de return in de tussenronde van de Champions League trainde Prestianni echter gewoon mee in het Bernabéu. Ook de geschorste trainer José Mourinho stond op het veld.

items.title: Williamson refuses to rule out strike action amid player overload concerns
items.summary: England captain concerned by relentless growth of game Ukraine World Cup qualifier set for Hill Dickinson Stadium England’s captain, Leah Williamson, has said she would never take the possibility of strike action off the table if players are not listened to over scheduling concerns. Speaking before England’s first two World Cup qualifiers, against Ukraine in Turkey and Iceland in Nottingham next week, Williamson said drastic action was “always a possibility” with talk of player overloading at the highest level a growing concern. Continue reading...

items.title: Senegal PM criticises fan prison sentences for Afcon final clashes
items.summary: Senegal's prime minister says it is "regrettable" that 18 of its football fans have been handed prison sentences related to disturbances at the 2025 Afcon final.

items.title: Why is Mayweather fighting Pacquiao again? Who is favourite? And is it a one-off?
items.summary: On Monday, Floyd Mayweather and Manny Pacquiao stunned the boxing world by announcing a professional rematch - 11 years after their first bout.

items.title: Brook thrives at No 3 as McCullum's 'mastermind' move pays off
items.summary: Many of Brendon McCullum's decisions were panned as England tumbled to a largely self-inflicted 4-1 Ashes defeat this winter - but his latest call reaped rewards.

items.title: Smith wins Players Championship in Leicester as Dobey produces nine-darter
items.summary: Ross Smith thrashed Chris Dobey 8-2 to win his seventh career Players Championship title

items.title: Middlesbrough peg back Leicester; Wrexham, Southampton lead - EFL on Sky LIVE!
Williamson 'wouldn't rule out' striking over scheduling
items.summary: England captain Leah Williamson says she would "never rule out" strike action to get players' messaging across to governing bodies about schedule concerns.
